events.title: China blocks Meta from acquiring AI startup Manus
events.summary: Meta said Monday that the transaction "complied fully with applicable law" and that it anticipates "an appropriate resolution to the inquiry."

events.title: Alleged Correspondents' Dinner shooter to appear in court. And, Charles III visits U.S.
events.summary: The suspect in the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting incident is set to appear in federal court today. And, King Charles III and Queen Camilla arrive in Washington today for a state visit.

events.title: Iran's flurry of diplomacy, as Trump insists U.S. has 'the cards'
events.summary: Iran's foreign minister arrived in Russia on Monday, after a whirlwind weekend of diplomacy, seeking to gain political leverage and foreign backing as peace talks with the U.S. remain on hold.

events.title: Palestinian officials hail local elections in a Gaza community and the West Bank
events.summary: Palestinian officials say local elections in Gaza and the West Bank mark a step toward a long-delayed presidential election. The Palestinian Authority hasn't held a presidential election in 21 years.

events.title: Egypt's Grand Museum opens after many delays
events.summary: We visit the new Grand Egyptian Museum, a spacious museum outside Cairo dedicated to displaying artifacts from ancient Egypt.  It has finally opened its doors after decades of planning and construction.

events.title: How China is responding to the stalling of talks between the U.S. and Iran
events.summary: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks to Zongyuan Zoe Liu, a senior fellow at the Council on Foreign Relations, about how China views the current crisis in the Middle East

events.title: Trump doubts shooter motivated by Iran war as peace talks on hold
events.summary: A shooting incident at the White House Correspondent's Dinner took focus away from the war in Iran, as Iran's foreign minister planned to return to Islamabad, the site of previous peace talks.

events.title: King Charles U.S. visit comes at tense moment in transatlantic relationship
events.summary: King Charles and Queen Camilla arrive Monday for a four day U.S. state visit. Some hope the royal touch can heal the transatlantic rift that's emerged under Trump.

events.title: In a rare interview, a leader of the world's largest right-wing group talks to NPR
events.summary: The second-in-command of the RSS, a Hindu nationalist organization in India, rarely speaks to the Western press. Here's what he said about his group's controversial history.

events.title: Mali reeling after coordinated attacks hit multiple cities
events.summary: Gunfire and explosions rocked Mali's capital and other key cities in one of the most significant coordinated attacks in years, as armed groups exploited worsening insecurity in the Sahel region.

events.title: Merz says US 'humiliated,' lacks strategy in Iran conflict
events.summary: Germany's Friedrich Merz says Iran has appeared more resilient than the US expected and warned that the conflict is escalating without a clear exit strategy. He said an "entire nation" has been humiliated by Tehran.

events.title: US news: Suspect in DC gala shooting to appear in court
events.summary: A suspect in the shooting at the White House Correspondents' Dinner is set to be formally charged in court. Plus, Britain's King Charles III is headed to the US after a security review cleared the planned trip.

events.title: Will the Iran war end Strait of Hormuz oil supremacy?
events.summary: The conflict has brutally exposed the energy market's Achilles' heel. While global powers China, India and the EU push renewables, Gulf leaders are advancing plans for new bypass pipelines to safeguard their oil empires.

events.title: Musk vs. OpenAI trial to get underway
events.summary: Two giants in the AI sector are set for a courtroom battle: Elon Musk (xAI) is taking on Sam Altmann (OpenAI). Musk has accused Altmann of betraying OpenAI's originally nonprofit mission.

events.title: Portugal's arms industry is taking off
events.summary: Portugal has built up a surprisingly large, independent arms industry over the past five years. But whether it can be truly autonomous with the US as a major ally remains to be seen.

events.title: Germany news: Rise in young people refusing military service
events.summary: Amid talk of a possible return of compulsory military service, more young Germans are applying for conscientious objector status. Meanwhile, Germany has become the world's fourth-largest military spender. DW has more.

events.title: India news: Delhi to get respite, heat wave persists in other parts
events.summary: Storms are expected in the capital, but severe heat across multiple states would continue, India's weather department said. DW has the latest.

events.title: Iran war deadline heats up Trump-Congress showdown
events.summary: A US president can order a military operation without congressional approval, but only for 60 days. After that they must seek formal approval from Congress. For the Iran war, this deadline expires on May 1.

events.title: Iran's Araghchi visits Russia as talks with US on hold
events.summary: Iran's foreign minister is continuing his diplomatic drive amid the war against his country with a trip to Russia. It comes after planned talks with the US in Pakistan were canceled.

events.title: SIPRI: Record arms spending, again
events.summary: Global military spending hit a record high in 2025. The Stockholm International Peace Research Institute, or SIPRI, warns the trend is likely to continue — and carries additional risks.

events.title: Russia, North Korea agree 'long-term' military cooperation
events.summary: Russia's defense minister and the chairman of the State Duma are both visiting North Korea. One helped inaugurate a memorial to North Korean soldiers killed fighting Ukraine, the other hashed out a new defense deal.

events.title: Colombia highway bomb attack kills at least 19
events.summary: Suspected guerillas carried out a deadly explosives attack on the Pan-American Highway in southwest Colombia. The region has been gripped by violence over the past few days.

events.title: Gunman was likely targeting Trump team, says official
events.summary: The suspect in the attempted attack at the White House Correspondents' dinner is believed to have been targeting US President Donald Trump and members of his administration.

events.title: Syria: First trial of Assad-era officials opens in Damascus
events.summary: A court in Damascus is trying one official in former President Bashar Assad's government in person, and several more in absentia. Assad and his brother are among the defendants. Both are thought to have fled to Russia.

events.title: Germany pensions will fall short of living standards: Merz
events.summary: Germany's state pension will only give "basic cover" in retirement, Chancellor Friedrich Merz has warned amid hot debate. How does the system compare globally?

events.title: Germany news: Söder calls for compulsory military service
events.summary: The Bavarian state premier has criticized the government's voluntary plan. Meanwhile, Chancellor Friedrich Merz condemned the shooting in Washington.

events.title: Sawe smashes 2-hour mark setting record at London Marathon
events.summary: Kenya's Sabastian Sawe has made history in London as the first man to run a marathon in under two hours. He beat the world record by more than a minute.

events.title: Mali: Fighting continues as Tuareg separatists claim Kidal
events.summary: A spate of attacks was coordinated by Tuareg separatists and jihadis with links to al-Qaeda. Among them, a car bomb in Bamako killed Mali's defense minister.

events.title: Queer as folk: The gay scandal that shook the German Empire
events.summary: Germany's last emperor, a gilded porcelain vase and a general who had a heart attack in a pink tutu: The Eulenburg Affair shook the German Empire to its core and paved the way for the downfall of the monarchy.

events.title: India news: Modi targets TMC in Bengal rally
events.summary: PM Modi addressed rallies in West Bengal, taking jibes at the opposition party TMC ahead of the second phase of the state poll. Meanwhile, fresh clashes have erupted in Manipur.

events.title: 40 years after Chernobyl: Pripyat today
events.summary: After the catastrophic accident in the nearby nuclear reactor, the city of Pripyat had to be completely evacuated. Some 50,000 people left their homes forever. DW visited the town with a former resident 40 years later.

events.title: Zelenskyy ready to hold Ukraine-Russia talks in Azerbaijan
events.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said his country is ready to hold talks with Russia in Azerbaijan. Meanwhile, Russian attacks killed several people across Ukraine.

events.title: Ukraine: Zelenskyy visits Saudi Arabia, Putin weighs G20 attendance
events.summary: Russian President Putin "may or not may not" attend the G20 in Miami in December, says the Kremlin. Meanwhile, after securing an EU loan, Ukrainian President Zelenskyy continued his diplomatic offensive in Saudi Arabia.

events.title: Is the far-right AfD misusing its parliamentary questions?
events.summary: The Alternative for Germany (AfD) has submitted thousands of official questions in state parliaments — a right of any elected party. But critics argue that their focus is meant to pressure political opponents.

events.title: The silent power of memes
events.summary: They are funny, provocative and sometimes tasteless. But memes are also powerful political tool, shaping our perception — usually without us noticing.

events.title: German economy growth forecasts halved
events.summary: Germany's economy continues to falter under the shock of soaring energy prices. In the wake of the Iran war, the German economy minister has had to cut her own forecast by half.

events.title: States gather in a new push to ditch coal, oil and gas
events.summary: At an unprecedented meeting in Colombia, delegates from more than 50 countries want to chart a practical path to wean the world off fossil fuels. Can they succeed where the UN climate talks have failed?

events.title: Meta, Microsoft purge jobs amid AI build-up
events.summary: Facebook's parent company said it would slash 10% of its workforce, while Microsoft is offering an early retirement scheme. The cuts come as both tech giants make massive investments in AI.

events.title: What is Europe's plan to secure the Strait of Hormuz?
events.summary: France and the UK are leading efforts to establish a multinational mission to safeguard shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Questions remain about the scope of the operation and whether it can bring lasting stability.

events.title: UK: Health data volunteered by 500,000 people to Biobank charity listed for sale on Alibaba in China
events.summary: The British government is investigating how health data volunteered to a charity for research purposes wound up for sale via three vendors on the Alibaba e-commerce site in China. One listing had 500,000 people's data.

events.title: Germany unveils first-ever military strategy for Bundeswehr
events.summary: For the first time in its history, the Bundeswehr has adopted a military strategy to prepare Germany for future threats. Large sections of the document are classified.

events.title: With its energy under fire, Ukraine turns to solar
events.summary: Since Chernobyl, Ukraine has lived in the shadow of nuclear disaster. Now, with Russia targeting its power grid, it's facing a new nuclear risk. The response is reshaping how the world thinks about energy in wartime.

events.title: Unpacking the 2026 Venice Biennale controversies and highlights
events.summary: Following the return of Russia to the Biennale, the EU said it will carry out its threat to cut its funding from the international art show.

events.title: A book lover's dream holiday in Scotland
events.summary: As UNESCO celebrates World Book Day on April 23, we visit a Scottish coastal town that was saved by books.

events.title: Bayern Munich on the brink of historic 'double treble'
events.summary: With Bundesliga titles in the bag and cup finals to come, Bayern's men and women have a chance to achieve something unique in Europe. The two teams dominate in Germany and appear united in their Champions League aims.

events.title: Denmark: Two trains collide injuring several
events.summary: Two trains have crashed into each other in Denmark, leaving several people critically injured, authorities have said.

events.title: UK, France agree 3‑year deal to curb Channel crossings
events.summary: For the first time, the deal links some UK payments to France to performance in stopping migrants. Funding will be halted after one year if targets are not met.

events.title: Germany's Merz: Climate protection must not hold economy back
events.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said the public would not accept climate policies that lead to deindustrialization, warning that they would hinder progress and innovation.

events.title: Germany's finance minister wants to scrap spousal tax splitting
events.summary: Highly controversial, but not well understood: German Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil wants to change the tax benefits for married couples.

events.title: Russia to block Kazakh oil flows to Germany via key pipeline
events.summary: Germany has confirmed that Russia will block flows of Kazakh oil to a vital refinery that supplies much of Berlin's energy needs from May 1.

events.title: EU unblocks €90 billion loan to Ukraine
events.summary: Envoys from EU member states have finally paved the way for a €90 billion loan to Ukraine that had long been blocked by Hungary. They also approved a 20th package of sanctions on Russia over its invasion of its neighbor.

events.title: The rise and fall of East Germany's Palace of the Republic
events.summary: From 1976 through 1990, the building in East Berlin served as a symbol of the communist state's power. The "People's Palace" was controversially destroyed — and yet remains present.

events.title: Ukraine says Russian oil to Hungary, Slovakia has restarted
events.summary: Energy companies in Ukraine and Hungary have both said that oil flows through the Druzhba pipeline from Russia via Belarus have restarted. Hungary and Slovakia expect the first supplies to arrive by Thursday.

events.title: Israeli ambassador: 'Only a strong Israel can achieve peace'
events.summary: In an interview with DW, Ron Prosor, Israel's ambassador to Germany, defends his country's military strikes against Iran and Lebanon. He also advises Germany's Chancellor Friedrich Merz to show more "tact."

events.title: Avoid men, German police association chief tells women
events.summary: Police association chief Dirk Peglow has stirred debate with his advice to women to avoid relationships with men for safety's sake. But his provocative tip is not without reason, as statistics show.

events.title: Could Iran war trigger bigger trade crisis than COVID?
events.summary: As the Iran war simmers, trade experts ask whether it could reshape supply chains more deeply than COVID-19, forcing companies to rethink strategies to counter further geopolitical shocks.

events.title: Iran war: Jet fuel crisis deepens as Lufthansa cuts flights
events.summary: The impact of jet fuel shortages because of the closure of the Strait of Hormuz is starting to burn. Lufthansa has canceled 20,000 flights and experts warn more cancellations and price hikes are on the way.

events.title: Record number of immigrants reside in EU in 2025
events.summary: The foreign‑born population of the EU grew to around 64 million in 2025. Germany remains the biggest host of immigrants in absolute terms, but some smaller nations have a much higher percentage of foreign-born residents.

events.title: How grievance turns to violence in a mass shooter's mind
events.summary: Attacks like last week's school shooting in Turkey can seem to come out of nowhere, but they rarely do. Instead, a buildup of grievance and fixation often precedes the the moment violent thoughts become violent acts.

events.title: UK moves to prohibit tobacco sales to anyone born after 2008
events.summary: A draft law in the UK to create a "smoke-free generation" by tobacco sales for anybody born after 2008 has cleared both houses of parliament. Only the king's signature remains for it to become law.

events.title: Mars: NASA's Curiosity rover finds new organic matter in Gale Crater
events.summary: NASA's Curiosity rover has identified more organic compounds as scientists investigate any potential ancient life signs on Mars. It was exploring the massive Gale Crater also believed to be a now-dry lake.

events.title: What course will Bulgaria’s new leader Rumen Radev take?
events.summary: The trained pilot, former general and ex-president says he will crack down on corruption and seek dialogue with Russia. But who is Rumen Radev, the man who swept to victory in Sunday’s election?

events.title: Apple switches CEO: What it means for the tech giant
events.summary: Apple's top hardware engineer John Ternus will lead the $4 trillion iPhone maker, as Tim Cook steps back. Will Apple chase rivals for the smartest intelligence, or deliver the best personal AI experience?

events.title: Hungary: Orban-era LGBTQ law infringes human rights, ECJ rules
events.summary: The EU's top court has found that Hungary's 2021 laws on issues including transgender and homosexual identity and sex offenses breach various EU laws. This includes Article 2 of EU Treaty, to protect human dignity.

events.title: Can data centers be green?
events.summary: Renewable energy is increasingly being used to supply power-hungry data centers. As the sector swells, much of the electricity demand is being met by polluting fossil fuels.

events.title: Rat poison found in baby food, extortion suspected
events.summary: Rat poison has been found in HiPP baby food jars across Austria, Czechia and Slovakia in what authorities suspect is a blackmail attempt.

events.title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple CEO, John Ternus to take over
events.summary: John Ternus, who has long focused ​on design and ‌products at Apple, will lead the iPhone maker as it gears up for an industry change spurred by artificial intelligence.

events.title: UK: Starmer says he would't have appointed Mandelson as US envoy if he'd known he failed vetting process
events.summary: Under-fire Prime Minister Keir Starmer told the House of Commons that neither he nor his ministers were told that the former ambassador to the US and Epstein associate Peter Mandelson had failed his security vetting.

events.title: Bundesliga debut by female coach marred by misogyny
events.summary: Marie-Louise Eta's historic debut as a head coach in the men's Bundesliga ended in defeat. While her appointment is a sign of progress, the reactions to it leave no doubt about how much is still to be done.

events.title: China's chip ambitions shake up global tech industry
events.summary: US curbs on advanced chips pushed China to focus on its own semiconductor ecosystem. And while it trails at the very cutting edge, China's "good‑enough" technology is fast powering much of the global economy.

events.title: The German refugees who found shelter in Yugoslavia
events.summary: After the Nazis seized power in 1933, many Germans escaped by fleeing abroad. Some ended up in what was then Yugoslavia. German historian Marie Janine Calic tells their story.

events.title: Bayern Munich beat Stuttgart to clinch Bundesliga title
events.summary: Bayern Munich are German league champions for a record-extending 35th time after beating Stuttgart 4-2. They have now won 13 of the past 14 Bundesliga titles.

events.title: Mines in the Strait of Hormuz: How dangerous are they?
events.summary: Iran says it has placed mines in the Straight of Hormuz to discourage independent traffic. How dangerous are naval mines, and what can be done to clear them?

events.title: Germany News: Neighbor attacked for pounding schnitzel too loudly
events.summary: Police in southern Germany were called in to settle a schnitzel dispute that had turned violent, with a man reportedly slapping his neighbor for pounding his schnitzel too loudly.

events.title: Climate change impacts India's harvest festivals
events.summary: As people across India celebrate traditional agrarian spring festivals, climate change has become an unwanted guest at the table. How are communities rising to the pressure on harvests, water, and rural life?

events.title: German crime figures: Are migrants unfairly targeted?
events.summary: Is it true that the crime rate in Germany is higher among immigrants? Statistics suggest as much, but numbers by themselves can be misleading. An expert explains what's behind them.

events.title: Hannover Messe 2026: Brazilian flair awaits visitors
events.summary: With war in Iran on hold, the world's largest industrial trade fair opens in Hannover. And Brazil, this year's partner country, is pulling out all stops to convince visitors it's a reliable technology leader.

events.title: The German who recovered from a stroke to seal Olympic first
events.summary: Kathrin Marchand was an elite rower for years before becoming a doctor. But a stroke at the age of 30 changed everything. Despite the challenges, she has now achieved something no other Olympian ever has.

events.title: Record fuel prices: How are governments responding?
events.summary: In Germany, a liter of diesel costs up to 40% more than before the Iran war and other countries have seen even bigger price jumps. What are governments around the world doing to combat record fuel prices?

events.title: Teen discovers first ancient Greek artifact found in Berlin
events.summary: A 13-year-old stumbled upon a coin from ancient Troy — an extremely rare find in Germany. Experts say it may offer new clues about little-known links between ancient Greece and northern Europe.

events.title: Chess: Uzbekistan's new star shows Asia's continued rise
events.summary: Javokhir Sindarov of Uzbekistan has earned the right to challenge India's Dommaraju Gukesh for the world title. Both men are under 21 and from Asia, underlining an ongoing demographic shift in top level chess.

events.title: Nueva Germania: The failed 'Aryan project' in Paraguay
events.summary: A new home for the "Aryan race." That was what German emigrants envisioned when they founded Nueva Germania in Paraguay in 1886. It failed back then but a version of the town still exists today.

events.title: Senegal: The Goethe-Institut opens its new building in Dakar
events.summary: The Goethe-Institut's new building in Dakar champions sustainable architecture, made from the earth. Star architect Francis Kere has blended local materials with contemporary design.

events.title: Anatomy of the clitoris: 3D images reveal its nerve system
events.summary: New 3D images reveal the clitoris’s complex nerve network, highlighting long‑standing gaps in medical understanding of the female body.

events.title: Top EU court rules online gamblers can sue for compensation if betting illegal in home country
events.summary: The ECJ has ruled that online gamblers can seek compensation if they lost money when gambling was illegal in their home country, even if the laws have since changed. This could pave the way for others to reclaim losses.

events.title: Germany-Israel divide deepens after Merz criticism
events.summary: The Israeli finance minister's sharp criticism of the German chancellor's views on Israel's settlement policy is just the latest sign of a growing estrangement. What's the current state of German-Israeli relations?

events.title: US jury finds Live Nation, Ticketmaster hold illegal monopoly
events.summary: The verdict will not bring immediate relief to concertgoers frustrated by high ticket prices, but could cost the company hundreds of millions of dollars.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Fans with disabilities 'excluded'
events.summary: Complaints that the 2026 World Cup, hosted largely by the USA, is a rip off have been widespread and constant. But it's not just the prices that are making it feel impossible for football fans with disabilities.

events.title: Germany news: Teacher group urges action over pupil violence
events.summary: A German teachers' group says decisive action is needed to counter a surge in violence in classrooms and corridors. Meanwhile, the Greens say a blanket speed limit on major highways would help save fuel.

events.title: Berlin hosts Sudan conference amid brutal, forgotten war
events.summary: The war in Sudan has been raging for the past three years, and a ceasefire is nowhere in sight. Participants at a conference of donors in Berlin hope at least to ease the suffering of the people caught in the middle.

events.title: German health minister announces billions in cutbacks
events.summary: The comprehensive reform package aims to plug a multibillion-euro hole in Germany's expensive health care system. Reforms include mandatory second opinions for costly surgeries and no more coverage for homeopathy.

events.title: Solar power in Morocco's desert: Bold vision, mixed results
events.summary: A massive solar tower in the Moroccan desert is the beacon of an ambitious push for a clean energy future. But fossil fuels and grid constraints stand in the way.

events.title: Women's workwear: When proper fit becomes a safety issue
events.summary: With limited options, many women in trades and industry have to wear men's workwear. But a poor fit causes discomfort and can compromise safety. Now, some firms are stepping up to design safer, better-fitting gear.

events.title: What we call sex work — and what it says about society
events.summary: A Bonn exhibition traces the cultural history of sex work and shows how the words used to describe it have shaped stigma, policy and lived experience.

events.title: Germany: Man found guilty in Pelicot-style rape case
events.summary: A Chinese student has been found guilty of drugging and raping a woman in Munich over a period of months. The perpetrator was a member of a Telegram chat group where men organized rapes and shared images of their crimes.

events.title: The world ditched wasteful toilets, the US stayed behind
events.summary: US toilets use far more water than many of their global counterparts. President Donald Trump is pushing to loosen water pressure standards, a move critics say would increase waste.

events.title: Lufthansa pilots launch 2-day strike, cabin crew say they will strike right after
events.summary: Pilots who are members of the Vereinigung Cockpit trade union are striking on Monday and Tuesday. As hundreds of flights were canceled, the UFO cabin crew union said it would also strike on Wednesday and Thursday.

events.title: Trump's Strait of Hormuz blockade hits Iran's oil trade
events.summary: A US naval blockade cuts off Iran's main source of hard currency by halting most of its oil exports. Will President Trump's pressure force Tehran back to negotiations or widen the conflict?

events.title: Merck's Keytruda: A lifesaving drug, a global divide
events.summary: A cross-border investigation by DW and ICIJ reveals how pricing and patents helped turn a life-saving medicine into one of the world's best selling drugs while leaving many patients struggling to access it.

events.title: UFOs: 'Most are reported by people out for an evening smoke'
events.summary: Hansjürgen Köhler has been a volunteer UFO hunter for more than 50 years. He sees his work as scientific investigation, but sometimes also as caring for others.

events.title: First gay rights movement: Berlin's wild 1920s queer history
events.summary: Before the Nazis took power, Berlin was a center of LGBTQ+ rights and queer culture, with research institutes, a flourishing nightlife and one of the earliest gay rights' movements.

events.title: NASA's billion-dollar space race goes into overdrive
events.summary: NASA's renewed push to get a human mission to the moon and Mars is neither cheap, nor for the faint of heart. But who ends up covering the costs and what does everyone still on Earth get out of all this?

events.title: Recycling's next big thing — or big bluff?
events.summary: An advanced recycling process claims it can handle hard-to-recycle plastics, like packaging. But critics say it's not worth the effort.

events.title: Has Coachella festival sold out?
events.summary: One of the world's biggest open-air music and art festivals has been hit by controversies and declining credibility — yet the world's biggest acts continue to headline.

events.title: Veteran German actor Mario Adorf dies aged 95
events.summary: Few actors had a career as rich as Mario Adorf. He played legendary Hollywood villains, wrote German TV history and still had film roles at age 90.

events.title: Artemis II crew emotional as mission prepares for reentry
events.summary: In less than 48 hours, the Orion spacecraft is due to splash down in the Pacific Ocean. Crew members have said what they saw in space will forever change how they see the world.

events.title: Hungary election: How football has helped Orban keep power
events.summary: Viktor Orban is a football fanatic but has long used the sport as a political tool, both in Hungary and abroad. With polls predicting election defeat, he could also lose his chance of hosting the Champions League final.

events.title: Norway's Telenor faces lawsuit for giving junta data from its customers in Myanmar
events.summary: A Swedish non-profit has filed a class action lawsuit against Norway's Telenor, accusing it of endangering customers in Myanmar by sharing their data with the junta. The company said it had no real choice.

events.title: How farmers are rising to a fertilizer shortfall
events.summary: The war in Iran has sparked a global fertilizer shortage and is threatening food security. What are farmers around the world doing to save their crops?

events.title: Should Western influencers promote Taliban‑run Afghanistan?
events.summary: In the race for clicks, likes and followers, some travel content creators are being criticized for promoting Afghanistan while ignoring the harsh reality of Taliban rule.

events.title: 60 years after the Indonesian mass killings: Is the Cold War back?
events.summary: Six decades after helping the Indonesian army crush one of the biggest communist parties in the world, the US is once again meddling in countries it considers a thorn in its flesh. Is history repeating itself?

events.title: Will AI doom office space to history?
events.summary: Artificial intelligence's potential for disruption is being felt across multiple industries. Now commercial real estate is feeling the heat from what analysts say is a "paradigm shift."

events.title: Homegrown energy is slashing US energy bills
events.summary: In times of skyrocketing energy prices, some US citizens are making big savings by producing electricity at home.

events.title: One in five young Germans plan to leave the country
events.summary: A recent study shows that as many as one in five Germans between the ages of 14 and 29 are actively thinking about moving to another country — and 40% plan to do so in the long term.

events.title: Can small nuclear reactors solve Europe's energy woes?
events.summary: Supporters say modular reactors are key to meeting energy goals, but critics say they're a costly distraction.

events.title: Mediterranean sharks are vanishing in a legal void
events.summary: Along Libya's coast, sharks — including breeding females — are being sold unchecked. With little enforcement and growing financial pressure on fishermen, vulnerable species are spiraling toward extinction.

events.title: 'Drill baby drill' ― Trump opens up nature to big energy
events.summary: America's beloved national parks and public lands face conservation rollbacks and sell-offs as the administration of US President Donald Trump pushes for fossil fuel and timber extraction.

events.title: Semenya hits out at impact of Olympic ban on DSD athletes
events.summary: Caster Semenya has had her gender scrutinized in public for years after winning two Olympic golds. She has hit out at what she sees as a policy that disproportionately affects athletes with sexual differences.

events.title: Wealth tax: Why are countries afraid to tax the ultrarich?
events.summary: Growing income inequality and public displays of billionaire power are increasing calls for new income and wealth taxes aimed at the world's richest individuals. Can wealth ever be taxed fairly?

events.title: How US 'Liberation Day' tariffs reshaped global trade
events.summary: One year after Trump's "Liberation Day" tariffs, global trade flows have shifted. Trade data shows which countries gained, which lost — and who is footing the bill.

events.title: UNESCO World Heritage sites facing the heat
events.summary: From Easter Island to the Great Wall of China, many of the world's most iconic cultural heritage sites are under threat from rising temperatures linked to climate change.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Iraq qualify but Iran doubts loom
events.summary: Iraq have overcome tough circumstances to reach the World Cup for the first time in 30 years, with the lineup now complete. But Iran's potential withdrawal still hangs over a tournament fraught with political issues.

events.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Trump's politics put off German fans
events.summary: Many German fans are torn on whether to travel to the US for this summer's major football tournament.

events.title: Tiger Woods 'stepping away' for treatment after DUI arrest
events.summary: Tiger Woods has announced a break from golf four days after he crashed his SUV and was arrested for driving under influence. Fans had been awaiting his return at the Masters this month.

events.title: Were Grandma and Grandpa Nazis?
events.summary: What did our forebears do during the Nazi era? A subject long shrouded in silence in German families. Now, the US National Archives has made millions of NSDAP membership cards available online.

events.title: FIFA World Cup: Which German players will make the final cut?
events.summary: Several players on Germany's national football team have really stood out during the squad's recent test matches ahead of this summer's major soccer tournament.

events.title: After the Iran war, how fast could global trade recover?
events.summary: The Iran war could end soon as a ceasefire goes into effect. But the true test will be how quickly energy, fertilizer and other supply chains can bounce back whenever the Strait of Hormuz is reopened.

events.title: Germany beat Ghana to complete confidence-boosting March
events.summary: A late winner from Stuttgart's Deniz Undav secured a second friendly win in a week for Germany. With the FIFA World Cup just 10 weeks away, Julian Nagelsmann's team will be starting to believe they can win it all.

events.title: Air Canada CEO to retire after English-only condolence furor
events.summary: Air Canada has announced that CEO Michael Rousseau will retire later this year. This follows public criticism of his failure to voice condolences in French as well as English after two pilots were killed in a collision.

events.title: The pleasure of books in the digital age
events.summary: The digitization of global literature has brought vast libraries to our fingertips. But the quiet power of a physical book offers a rare escape from constant digital noise.

events.title: Amnesty flags FIFA World Cup human rights risks
events.summary: The human rights group has sounded the alarm about risks faced by visitors to the 2026 FIFA World Cup. It goes as far as to describe the situation in the United States as a "human rights emergency."

events.title: Caught between the UK and EU: What’s next for young Scots?
events.summary: So much of Scotland’s history has been forged by its relationship to England and by one central question: union or independence? Brexit has given the independence movement new momentum, and with Scottish elections coming up in May, the question is back at the centre of the debating table. In this new episode of "Growing up in Europe", ENTR travelled to Scotland to ask young people where they see the future of their country: in the UK, or on its own?

events.title: Amsterdam becomes world's first capital to ban fossil fuel ads
events.summary: Cities around the globe are clearing their billboards of adverts for flights, cruise ships and petrol cars in a bid to reduce global warming. Amsterdam is the latest city to join the movement, becoming the first capital in the world to approve a legal ban on fossil fuel advertisements. Following the historic win, Dutch activists are now calling for restrictions to be enforced on a national level. FRANCE 24's Valérie Dekimpe and Alexandra Renard report.

events.title: Michael Jackson: 'Untouchable' icon, sexual predator or both?
events.summary: PRESS REVIEW – Monday, April 27: Michael Jackson's biopic is enjoying a strong debut, despite numerous sexual abuse accusations. But first: papers discuss conspiracy theories following the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner shooting. Plus: in some good news, a chubby sea lion has won the hearts of San Francisco locals and a German nun discovers the joy of eating a kebab.

events.title: Gunman sought to kill Trump, cabinet at gala dinner
events.summary: Investigators on Sunday said the gunman who tried to storm a gala dinner attended by Donald Trump planned to assassinate the US president and multiple top officials. The suspect, widely named as Cole Allen, 31, had written in a note shared with his family shortly before the attack that his targets would be "prioritized from highest-ranking to lowest."

events.title: Israel, Hezbollah trade accusations over Lebanon truce violations
events.summary: Lebanon's health ministry said Israeli strikes on the country's south on Sunday killed 14 people, the deadliest day since a ceasefire in the Israel-Hezbollah war came into force over a week ago. It came as Israel and the Iran-backed group traded fresh accusations of breaching the fragile truce, with Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu saying the military was "vigorously" targeting Hezbollah and the group vowing to keep responding to "violations".

events.title: Musk and OpenAI CEO Altman head to court in high-stakes showdown over AI
events.summary: Technology tycoons Elon Musk and Sam Altman are poised to face off in a high-stakes trial revolving around the alleged betrayal, deceit and unbridled ambition that blurred the bickering billionaires' once-shared vision for the development of artificial intelligence. The trial centres on the 2015 birth of ChatGPT maker OpenAI as a nonprofit startup primarily funded by Musk before evolving into a capitalistic venture now valued at $852 billion.

events.title: 'This keeps happening': US press gala shooting latest sign of rising political violence
events.summary: A California man accused of storming a gala dinner attended by President Donald Trump was to appear in court Monday over a shooting that marks at least the third time in three years that Trump has come under threat by an attacker, raising fresh concerns about security, and the growing pattern of political violence across the country.

events.title: Trump's security under scrutiny after shooting
events.summary: U.S. law enforcement officials are reassessing security arrangements after a gunman opened fire near the White House Correspondents' Association dinner, raising questions about how he was able to get so close to an event attended by President Donald Trump, cabinet members and lawmakers. France 24's Fraser Jackson has the latest from Washington.

events.title: Israeli strikes kill 14 in Lebanon despite ceasefire
events.summary: Israeli ​strikes killed 14 people and wounded 37 on Sunday, Lebanon's health ministry said, as the Israeli military warned residents to leave seven towns beyond the "buffer zone" it occupied before a ceasefire that has failed to fully halt hostilities. France 24's correspondent in Jerusalem Noga Tarnopolsky has the latest.

events.title: Mali defence minister killed as jihadis and rebels launch major assault
events.summary: Mali Defence Minister Sadio Camara was ​killed in an attack on his residence, the government said on Sunday, a high-profile fatality during coordinated assaults staged the previous day by insurgents including the West Africa al Qaeda affiliate.

events.title: King Charles' visit to US goes ahead despite Washington shooting
events.summary: King Charles' state visit to the U.S. will go ahead as planned on ​Monday despite a shooting at a White House dinner attended by President Donald Trump, Buckingham Palace said after discussions with U.S. officials. The four-day trip - which is to include a private meeting with Trump and an address to Congress marking 250 years since U.S. independence — is intended to reinforce the strained U.S.-British relationship ‌amid differences over the Iran war.

events.title: Investigators probe motives of US press gala shooting suspect
events.summary: Investigators on Sunday said the gunman who tried to storm a gala dinner attended by Donald Trump planned to assassinate the US president and multiple top officials, as they continue to probe suspects exact motives. France 24's Andrew Hillier has the latest.

events.title: Trump calls suspect in press dinner attack 'pretty sick guy'
events.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump said on Sunday that the suspect accused of trying to attack administration officials at a ​black-tie gala on Saturday night was a "pretty sick guy" who had been flagged to law enforcement by family members. Trump said in TV interviews that the suspect, whom an official identified as 31-year-old Cole Tomas Allen, of Torrance, California, had posted what Trump described as an "anti-Christian" manifesto.

events.title: 'Unable to cope': Malian army, Wagner 'overstretched' by Tuareg separatists and jihadist militants
events.summary: Oliver Farry is pleased to welcome Paul Melly, Consulting Fellow on the Africa Program at Chatham House. According to Melly, what we are witnessing is the convergence of multiple fractures: political, territorial, and military. The recent attacks illustrate a dramatic expansion of jihadist operational reach into the very heart of state power. While the Malian state might not be collapsing outright, its authority is stretched dangerously thin across Africa's 8th largest country. The withdrawal of int'l actors, combined with the limits of partnerships, has exposed structural vulnerabilities.

events.title: Kim reaffirms North Korean support for Russia's 'sacred' Ukraine war
events.summary: North Korean leader Kim Jong-un reaffirmed support for Russia's invasion of Ukraine and vowed to help Moscow achieve victory in its "sacred war", state media reported Monday, after Russian Defence Minister Andrey Belousov met with Kim on Sunday. Pyongyang has sent missiles, munitions and thousands of troops to assist Russia in Ukraine, and analysts say Moscow is sending financial aid, military technology, food and energy to the diplomatically isolated country in return.

events.title: Kenya's Sabastian Sawe becomes first man to run a marathon in under two hours
events.summary: Kenyan athlete Sabastian Sawe entered the history books by becoming the first man to run a marathon in under two hours. He won the London race in 1 hour, 59 minutes, and 30 seconds.

events.title: China warns EU over ‘Made in Europe’ plan, vows countermeasures
events.summary: China on Monday criticised European Union plans to shield key industries from Chinese competition, warning it would take countermeasures if the measures are adopted. The EU’s proposed “Made in Europe” rules would require companies seeking public funds in sectors including cars, green technology and steel to meet local content thresholds.

events.title: Middle East war live: Lebanon’s Aoun says will not accept 'humiliating' deal in Israel talks
events.summary: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun said Monday that he will not accept a "humiliating agreement" with Israel, and accused those who drew Lebanon into war of "treason" in an implicit rebuke to Iran-backed Hezbollah. Aoun said his goal was to "reach an end to the state of war" as both Israel and Hezbollah traded accusations of truce violations. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

events.title: Elon Musk’s lawsuit against OpenAI over nonprofit claims heads to trial
events.summary: Tech billionaire Elon Musk’s lawsuit accusing OpenAI of abandoning its non-profit mission on Monday advances with jury selection in California. The case pits the world’s richest person against a startup he once backed and now rivals in the artificial intelligence sector.

events.title: King Charles III begins US visit despite press gala shooting, transatlantic tensions
events.summary: Britain's King Charles III and Queen Camilla began a four-day visit to the United States on Monday to honour historic ties between the two countries as the US marks its 250th anniversary. The trip will take place despite simmering transatlantic tensions over the Iran war, and a shooting two days earlier at a dinner attended by US President Donald Trump.

events.title: Global military spending hits record $2.9 trillion in 2025 amid growing insecurity
events.summary: Global military spending climbed to nearly $2.9 trillion in 2025, marking an 11th straight year of growth as rising insecurity and rearmament boosted defence budgets, researchers said on Monday. The United States, China and Russia accounted for $1.48 trillion, over half the total, SIPRI said.

events.title: Syria begins first trial for ousted Assad, allies
events.summary: A Syrian court held its first hearing on Sunday in the trial of ousted president Bashar al-Assad and senior officials from his former government, state media said. Assad and his brother Maher al-Assad, who fled Syria, are being tried in absentia, while former security official Atif Najib appeared in court in handcuffs.

events.title: A tutor with a manifesto: What we know about the US press gala shooting suspect
events.summary: The suspect in the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner shooting, widely named as Cole Tomas Allen, is a tutor with a degree in mechanical engineering who was opposed to President Donald Trump's policies, according to US media reports. In a manifesto sent to his family, the 31-year-old California man described himself as a "Friendly Federal Assassin" and apologised to his family and other "non-targeted people".

events.title: Iran’s top diplomat leaves Pakistan for Russia, Trump says Tehran can talk by phone
events.summary: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi on Sunday squeezed in a trip to Oman before returning to Pakistan and then heading to Moscow for talks in a frantic diplomatic shuffle as mediators pressed to reignite talks between Tehran and the US. But President Donald Trump stuck with his decision to scrap a planned trip by this envoys to Pakistan, saying Iran and the US could talk by phone instead.

events.title: Lightning strikes in Bangladesh kill 14 - including 10-year-old boy
events.summary: A 10-year-old boy is reported to have been killed in lightning strikes which left at least 14 people dead in Bangladesh.

events.title: Kim Jong Un speaks at memorial for North Korean soldiers killed fighting for Russia
events.summary: Kim Jong Un threw dirt over the remains of one dead soldier at the opening of a memorial museum for his compatriots who died fighting alongside Russia in the Ukraine war.

events.title: Worker killed at Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant after drone strike, says Russia
events.summary: A worker at the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power ​station has been killed in a ​Ukrainian drone attack, according to Russian ​forces, which control the plant.

events.title: Mali defence minister killed during major assault by insurgents
events.summary: Mali's defence minister has been killed during co-ordinated attacks on towns and military bases by insurgents, including al Qaeda-linked fighters, according to the government.

events.title: What we know about suspected gunman at Trump dinner
events.summary: The suspect who stormed through a security checkpoint and shot a Secret Service agent at the White House correspondents' dinner in Washington DC was armed with multiple weapons and knives, police say.

events.title: Benjamin Netanyahu's biggest rivals merge in bid to oust his government
events.summary: Two of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's biggest rivals have announced they are joining forces to attempt to oust his coalition government.

events.title: Archbishop of Canterbury prays with Pope Leo in historic meeting
events.summary: Dame Sarah Mullally, the first female Archbishop of Canterbury, has prayed with Pope Leo XIV at the Vatican - a meeting that would have been unthinkable just a few years ago.

events.title: Mystery surrounds fate of detained journalist six months after fall of key Sudan city
events.summary: Six months have passed since the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF) captured the Sudanese regional capital Al Fashir and shared dozens of videos documenting their massacre in the city.

events.title: Trump claims shooting would 'never have happened' if ballroom had been built
events.summary: Donald Trump has claimed a shooting at a White House correspondents' dinner would have been avoided had his proposed ballroom been built.

events.title: Terrorists launch simultaneous attacks in Mali, authorities say
events.summary: Terrorists have carried out attacks in various locations across Mali, according to the country's army.

events.title: Germany suspects Russia is behind Signal phishing that targeted top officials
events.summary: The German government suspects Russia is behind phishing attacks on Signal targeting high-ranking politicians, military personnel, and journalists

events.title: Suspected pirates seize vessel off Somalia, the second hijacking incident in days
events.summary: A cargo vessel flying the flag of St. Kitts and Nevis has been hijacked off Somalia’s coast

events.title: Gunmen attack orphanage in north-central Nigeria, abduct 23 pupils; 15 later rescued
events.summary: Gunmen have raided an illegally operated orphanage in north-central Nigeria, abducting 23 pupils

events.title: Pope Leo XIV vows to keep working to overcome differences with Anglicans in meeting with their its female leader
events.summary: Pope Leo XIV vows to keep working to overcome differences with Anglicans in meeting with their its female leader

events.title: Russian drone attack wounds 14 while Ukrainian drones kill 2 in Russia-held area
events.summary: Authorities in Ukraine say a Russian drone attack on Ukraine’s southern city of Odesa has wounded 14 people including two children

events.title: Taiwan court sentences ex-Tokyo Electron staff to 10 years in TSMC trade secrets case
events.summary: A court in Taiwan has sentenced a former employee of Tokyo Electron to 10 years in prison for stealing trade secrets from TSMC

events.title: At least 42 people killed in eastern Chad during clashes over water resources
events.summary: At least 42 people have been killed in eastern Chad after a clash between two families over a water point escalated into a cycle of reprisals

events.title: Archbishop of Canterbury meets with pope and prays at the Vatican
events.summary: Pope Leo XIV has vowed to keep working to overcome differences with the Anglican Communion

events.title: Energy shock ripples through kitchens and forests in Africa and South Asia
events.summary: Energy shocks linked to the Iran war are pushing households across Africa and South Asia back to charcoal and firewood as cleaner cooking methods become more expensive and unreliable

events.title: North Korea opens memorial museum for troops killed in Russia-Ukraine war
events.summary: North Korea has opened a memorial museum for its soldiers killed while fighting for Russia against Ukraine

events.title: Magnitude 6.1 earthquake strikes in northern Japan
events.summary: A magnitude 6.2 earthquake shook part of northern Japan early Monday, but there was no damage or casualties

events.title: Palestinian authorities call local elections in a Gaza community and the West Bank a success
events.summary: Palestinian authorities say local elections in Gaza and the West Bank are a success and mark a step toward a long-delayed presidential election

events.title: Death toll from bus bombing in southwest Colombia rises to 20 during a wave of violence
events.summary: Officials in Colombia say the number of people killed in a bombing in a volatile region in the country's southwest has risen to 20

events.title: With goals of Iran war unfulfilled, Netanyahu's government faces unhappy public as elections loom
events.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu sold a vision to Israelis as the country entered the war with Iran and invaded Lebanon

events.title: Separatists in Mali say government troops and Russian mercenaries left the northern city of Kidal after rebel attacks
events.summary: Separatists in Mali say government troops and Russian mercenaries left the northern city of Kidal after rebel attacks

events.title: Two former Israeli prime ministers join forces against Netanyahu in upcoming elections
events.summary: Two Israeli political heavyweights plan to join forces in upcoming elections.

events.title: Strikes in Ukraine and Russia kill at least 16 on Chernobyl anniversary
events.summary: Strikes across Ukraine, Russian-occupied territory and Russia have killed at least 16 people

events.title: Syria’s first public trial of Assad-era officials opens in Damascus
events.summary: Syria has opened its first public trial of officials linked to former President Bashar Assad

events.title: Malian defense chief is killed as jihadis and rebels seize towns and military bases
events.summary: Malian Minister of Defense Gen. Sadio Camara has been killed in an attack as jihadi and rebel forces seized towns and military bases across the West African country

events.title: Car bomb explodes outside Northern Ireland police station
events.summary: Bombers have targeted a police station in Northern Ireland for the second time in less than a month

events.title: Despite Russia’s war, one Ukrainian city still gathers for midnight Chernobyl vigil
events.summary: Ukrainians gathered in the city of Slavutych to remember those affected by the Chernobyl nuclear disaster 40 years ago

events.title: Growing amateur choir brings joy and community to hundreds in Serbia
events.summary: An amateur pop choir in Serbia is finding unexpected success across the country

events.title: Iran's top diplomat briefly returns to Pakistan but Trump says the sides can talk by phone
events.summary: Iran's foreign minister has briefly visited Islamabad again as Pakistan's leaders work to restart ceasefire talks between Tehran and Washington

events.title: Philippine officials say 2 Americans among suspected communist rebels killed in clash with troops
events.summary: Philippine officials say two Americans were among 19 suspected communist guerrillas, who were killed in clashes with Filipino troops in a central province a week ago

events.title: The threat of light pollution puts the world’s darkest skies in the Atacama Desert at risk
events.summary: The Atacama Desert in Chile offers some of the clearest views of the universe, thanks to its dry climate, high altitude and isolation from light pollution

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 27 April: Fighting continues in Lebanon; Security Council to debate maritime security
events.summary: The recently extended ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon remains under strain as violence continues, with civilian deaths and injuries reported in Israeli strikes over the weekend. Meanwhile, the UN Security Council is set to hold a high-level debate on maritime security amid ongoing concerns over the safety of key global waterways. Stay with us for live updates.

events.title: Chokepoints and conflict: How the Hormuz crisis is exposing global shipping vulnerabilities
events.summary: The blockading of ships in the Strait of Hormuz as a result of the conflict between the United States and Iran has demonstrated how ships and seafarers have become “leverage in geopolitical disputes,” according to the head of the UN’s International Maritime Organization (IMO).

events.title: Renewables rising, Part 2: Seeking stability amid volatile fossil fuel markets
events.summary: As global energy markets remain volatile and the price of oil high due to instability and conflict in the Middle East, more countries are looking at investing in renewables to shield their economies from the shocks caused by fossil fuel reliance, rein in import bills – and meet key climate action targets.

events.title: Mali: Guterres calls for international solutions to curb spread of violent extremism in the Sahel
events.summary: There are reports of continuing clashes in Mali on Sunday, a day after a series of coordinated attacks across the landlocked African nation against Government forces by extremists and northern separatist rebels.

events.title: South Pacific islanders strive to rescue their home from toxic legacy
events.summary: More than 80 years after World War Two, the Solomon Islands remain one of the most heavily mine-contaminated places in the Pacific.

events.title: Renewables rising, Part 1: How four countries are reshaping energy security
events.summary: The ongoing insecurity in the Middle East, where a large proportion of the world’s oil and gas is extracted, is forcing many countries to focus on how they can ensure access to cheap and reliable energy supplies primarily through the use of renewable power sources.

events.title: A race for rights: How sport is helping protect girls in Uganda

events.title: Rising nuclear risks put global security pact to the test at UN Headquarters

events.summary: The price of oil jumped and stocks fell slightly Monday as traders digested renewed tensions between the U.S. and Iran over the critical Strait of Hormuz.U.S.

events.title: Israeli soldier seen striking Jesus statue in Lebanon, sparking condemnation

events.title: U.K. probes shadowy Islamic group amid arson spree at Jewish sites
events.summary: After a spate of arson attacks targeting Jewish sites in London, British authorities are investigating a shadowy online group with possible ties to Iran.
